evidence.text: Unit No:  [**Numeric Identifier 4669**] Admission Date: [**2167-8-11**] Discharge Date: [**2167-8-15**] Date of Birth:  [**2091-6-8**] Sex:  M Service:   HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:  Mr. [**Known lastname **] is a 76-year-old man with a history of hypertension, dyslipidemia, and coronary artery disease (status post inferior myocardial infarction with ventricular fibrillation arrest in [**2156**]) who has done well with only six episodes of exertional angina until then.

evidence.display: Three days ago, the patient woke up at 3:00 a.m. with severe midsternal chest pa
evidence.text: Three days ago, the patient woke up at 3:00 a.m. with severe midsternal chest pain radiating to the left arm.

evidence.display: It was similar to the symptoms of his prior myocardial infarction.
evidence.text: It was similar to the symptoms of his prior myocardial infarction.

evidence.display: It was not associated with nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, or shortness of breath.
evidence.text: It was not associated with nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, or shortness of breath.

evidence.display: The patient presented to [**Hospital **] Hospital within one half hour of the on
evidence.text: The patient presented to [**Hospital **] Hospital within one half hour of the onset of the pain where he was treated with sublingual nitroglycerin, oxygen, morphine, and heparin.

evidence.display: The patient became pain free and remained so for the remainder of his hospitaliz
evidence.text: The patient became pain free and remained so for the remainder of his hospitalization.

evidence.display: The patient ruled in for a myocardial infarction with a positive troponin level
evidence.text: The patient ruled in for a myocardial infarction with a positive troponin level of 2.87 and a creatine kinase of 426.

evidence.display: Cardiac catheterization performed on the day prior to admission at [**Hospital *
evidence.text: Cardiac catheterization performed on the day prior to admission at [**Hospital **] Hospital demonstrated a small LIMA, total occlusion of the SVG to OM,  and diseased segments of the SVG to diagonal to obtuse marginal.

evidence.display: The ejection fraction was normal.
evidence.text: The ejection fraction was normal.

evidence.display: The LV end-diastolic pressure was 19, right atrial pressure was 13, and pulmonar
evidence.text: The LV end-diastolic pressure was 19, right atrial pressure was 13, and pulmonary artery pressure 33/10.

evidence.display: The patient was transported to [**Hospital1 188**] on the day of admission where
evidence.text: The patient was transported to [**Hospital1 188**] on the day of admission where he underwent stenting of the saphenous vein graft to diagonal and obtuse marginal with a PercuSurge distal protection device.

evidence.display: The patient experienced his typical anginal symptoms during the procedure and fo
evidence.text: The patient experienced his typical anginal symptoms during the procedure and for approximately one hour status post procedure.

evidence.display: The patient denied a history of shortness of breath, dyspnea on exertion, paroxy
evidence.text: The patient denied a history of shortness of breath, dyspnea on exertion, paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea, orthopnea, palpitations, lightheadedness, syncope, or presyncope.

evidence.text: PHYSICAL EXAMINATION ON PRESENTATION:  Vital signs revealed his blood pressure was 100 to 110/60, his heart rate was 40 to 50 (without ectopy), and he was saturating 100 percent on room air.

evidence.text: Neck revealed no jugular venous distention.

evidence.text: Cholesterol was 154, his high-density lipoprotein was 30, and his low-density lipoprotein was 90.

evidence.text: PERTINENT RADIOLOGY-IMAGING:  An electrocardiogram revealed sinus bradycardia with a rate in the 50s.

evidence.display: There were small Q waves and ST-T wave sloping in III and aVF.
evidence.text: There were small Q waves and ST-T wave sloping in III and aVF.

evidence.text: There was a 1-mm ST segment depressions in V2 through V4.

evidence.display: SUMMARY OF HOSPITAL COURSE:  As noted above, the patient was taken to the Cardia
evidence.text: SUMMARY OF HOSPITAL COURSE:  As noted above, the patient was taken to the Cardiac Catheterization Laboratory for stenting procedure.

evidence.display: He was taken to the Cardiac Catheterization Laboratory.
evidence.text: He was taken to the Cardiac Catheterization Laboratory.

evidence.display: The patient complained of persistent chest pain where he had three stents placed
evidence.text: The patient complained of persistent chest pain where he had three stents placed.

evidence.display: Then the patient dropped his blood pressure significantly in the SVG to first di
evidence.text: Then the patient dropped his blood pressure significantly in the SVG to first diagonal to first obtuse marginal jump graft  90's with prominent ST depressions in V1, two hepicoat stents mid and distally, one cypher in the ostium.

evidence.display: At this time a thrombus was discovered at the site of intervention and distal st
evidence.text: At this time a thrombus was discovered at the site of intervention and distal stent was occluded.

evidence.display: Post procedure, the patient developed recurrent angina with more prominent ST de
evidence.text: Post procedure, the patient developed recurrent angina with more prominent ST depressions in V1 through V4, ST elevations in I and aVL, with a decrease in blood pressures to systolics in the 90s when the nitroglycerin drip was increased.

evidence.display: The patient was taken back to the Cardiac Catheterization Laboratory where a thr
evidence.text: The patient was taken back to the Cardiac Catheterization Laboratory where a thrombus was found at the site of intervention, and the distal stent was occluded.

evidence.display: The patient underwent angioplasty with two stent placements distally and adminis
evidence.text: The patient underwent angioplasty with two stent placements distally and administration of Integrelin, clopidogrel, and intracoronary TPA.

evidence.display: An intraaortic balloon pump was placed to maximize coronary blood flow.
evidence.text: An intraaortic balloon pump was placed to maximize coronary blood flow.

evidence.display: After this procedure the patient did well and was able to be weaned off the ball
evidence.text: After this procedure the patient did well and was able to be weaned off the balloon pump.

evidence.display: The patient was continued on aspirin, beta blocker, ACE inhibitor, Plavix, and s
evidence.text: The patient was continued on aspirin, beta blocker, ACE inhibitor, Plavix, and statin.

evidence.display: The patient did develop a moderate retroperitoneal hematoma requiring a transfus
evidence.text: The patient did develop a moderate retroperitoneal hematoma requiring a transfusion of blood, though this was stable.

evidence.display: In addition, the patient had episodes of nonsustained ventricular tachycardia fo
evidence.text: In addition, the patient had episodes of nonsustained ventricular tachycardia for which he will follow up with Electrophysiology for further risk stratification.
